content.problemStatement:
Write a program to print the length of each word of the sentence given below as well as the length of the whole sentence.

**"Coding on CodeChef"**

Note:There is spacing between the words and the number, consider it while printing the outputs.

**Note:** Please print the outputs in the same format as given below.


Output Format
Coding - 6

on - 2

CodeChef - 8

Coding on CodeChef - 18
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. No further input is required for each test case.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print four lines exactly as shown:
Coding - 6
on - 2
CodeChef - 8
Coding on CodeChef - 18
Print the blocks consecutively for multiple test cases, each line terminated by a newline.

sampleTestCases.explanation: T=1, so the four required lines are printed once.
T=2, the block is printed twice consecutively.

sampleTestCases.output:
Coding - 6
on - 2
CodeChef - 8
Coding on CodeChef - 18
Coding - 6
on - 2
CodeChef - 8
Coding on CodeChef - 18
Coding - 6
on - 2
CodeChef - 8
Coding on CodeChef - 18
sampleTestCases.explanation: T=3, the block is printed three times.

solution.approach: The required output does not depend on any computation; it is a fixed set of four lines. Read the integer T, then repeat the four‑line block T times. This yields O(T) time and O(1) auxiliary space (apart from the output buffer).
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑size block is printed for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few constant‑size strings are stored.
